title: Tail-recursive list reversal (accumulator) is correct
statement_informal: The O(n) list reversal via an accumulator is correct: rapp l acc = rev l ++ acc, hence fast_rev l = rapp l [] = rev l. (The naive rev is quadratic through ++; this is the linear tail-recursive one.)

cost_model.notes: rapp does one cons per element (linear, tail-recursive). Correctness via rapp l acc = rev l ++ acc (induction + app_assoc), then app_nil_r.

references: The accumulator (tail-recursive) list reversal — linear, vs the naive O(n^2) rev built from append.
